key_insight: The paper extends large-step gradient descent theory from isolated flat minima to manifolds of flat minima and vector-valued least-squares, while preserving a normal form and convergence guarantees.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more realistic theoretical basis for using step sizes larger than classical stability limits in overparameterized models such as deep matrix factorization and regression.
method: Generalize the large-step GD normal form to vector-valued outputs and to neighborhoods of flat-minimum manifolds.
Prove three convergence theorems in the broader setting, extending prior scalar-output, isolated-minimum results.
Handle a singular PDE in the analysis via a new method and derive manifold structure results for deep matrix factorization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports new theoretical results and structural theorems, but no experiments or benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: The guarantees are local and rely on specific overparameterized least-squares and manifold-regularity assumptions, so direct transfer to general deep networks may be limited.
paper_title: Dynamics of Gradient Descent with Large Step Size Near a Manifold of Flat Minima
